title: Mapania grandis
taxonomy: Mapania grandis is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Mapania and is native to Southeast Asia. The exact classification within the family Cyperaceae is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade monocots, order Poales, family Cyperaceae, genus Mapania, species M. grandis. This classification indicates that it is a monocot plant with a specific arrangement of leaves and flowers. Within the genus Mapania, M. grandis is one of several species that share similar characteristics.

intro: Mapania grandis is a type of aquatic plant, specifically a sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress.
